profile.profile_text:
First Name	Mikaela
Last Name	Moskowitz
Email	mikaelamoskowitz55@gmail.com
Current Status	Early stage professional (< 5 years)
Job Type Preference	Full Time Role
3. What types of roles are you targeting right now?	Business Operations Analyst, Process Transformation / Process Improvement Analyst, Strategy Analyst (Internal / Corporate Strategy), Post-Merger Integration (PMI) Analyst / Associate, Internal Consulting / Transformation Analyst, Operations & Strategy Analyst
Secondary or adjacent roles you would consider	Business Analyst, Internal Consultant or Transformation Enablement Analyst, Change Management or Organizational Development Analyst, Implementation / Solutions Analyst (non-sales), International or Cross-Border Operations Analyst
Business and Finance	Asset Management Consulting (Management/Strategy) Financial Services (General) Private Equity Venture Capital Wealth Management Professional Services (General)
Technology	FinTech eCommerce
Real Estate and Built Environment	Commercial Real Estate Real Estate Development Real Estate Private Equity
Healthcare and Life Sciences	Healthcare Services
Consumer, Retail & Lifestyle	Hospitality Travel and Tourism
Industrial, Manufacturing and Energy	Manufacturing Aerospace and Defense Supply Chain and Logistics
Startups and Emerging Companies	Startups (Growth Stage) Small/Founder-Led Companies
Specific companies you are particularly interested in (max 10)	Accenture, Deloitte, SAP, ServiceNow (potentially), Airbnb
5. Are there any roles or industries you D O N O T want?	Sales or commission-based roles, highly technical finance roles (accounting, financial reporting, valuation-heavy positions), data or analytics roles requiring programming, roles with primary focus on coding, software engineering, or technical development, aggressive, high-pressure finance environments with limited collaboration or mentorship, administrative roles without strategic, cross-functional work
How open are you to non-obvious entry points?	Somewhat open
6. Location preferences	Boston, Miami, Orlando, Charlotte, Chicago... essentially East Coast
7. Timeline for starting work	Fall 2026
8. What do you believe are your strongest skills right now?	My strongest skills are systems thinking, communication, and cross-functional coordination. I am good at understanding how different parts of a business fit together and identifying where processes, roles, or handoffs can be improved. I can be good at explaining complex or unfamiliar concepts in a clear, human way. Either in international settings or differings cultural environments. I can work well in ambiguity, take initiative when structure is missing, and constantly work as a bridging between finance and all of the departments of a company.
9. What about your job search gives you most concern?	My main concern is interview performance. My experience has been broad and cross-functional, so I find it challenging to present my strengths concisely and with confidence in interview settings—both in finance-labeled roles and in more strategy or operations-focused interviews. I want support in sharpening how I communicate my value, structure my answers, and perform confidently in interviews so that my skills in problem-solving, systems thinking, and communication come through clearly
10. How do you prefer feedback delivered?	Balanced (honest but encouraging)
Resume Paste	Mikaela Moskowitz
617.306.5288 | mikaelamoskowitz55@gmail.com | linkedin.com/in/mikaela-moskowitz
SUMMARY

Process transformation professional focused on modernizing finance, accounting, & operational systems in complex, regulated environments. Proven record leading end-to-end process redesign, close optimization, & cross-functional change initiatives across global organizations. U.S. citizen actively relocating to Orlando, FL.
CORE COMPETENCIES

Finance & Accounting Process Optimization | End-to-End Process Redesign & Standardization | Cross-Functional Transformation & Stakeholder Alignment | KPI Definition, Reporting, & Performance Tracking | Process Mapping, Documentation, & RACI Design | 
Change Management | ERP & Systems Enablement | Operational Controls, Governance, & Audit Readiness | 
Post-Merger Integration & Scalable Operating Models
RELEVANT EXPERIENCE

TTIS Germany GmbH						     				     	            Berlin, Germany
Post-Merger Integration Specialist 							      		       Dec 2024 – Present 
Lead cross-functional post-merger integration of two acquired entities, standardizing finance, HR, purchasing, & administrative processes to improve scalability & control.
Redesigned & operationalized the month-end close, establishing clear RACI ownership & reducing close cycle time by ~67%.
Re-engineered end-to-end project lifecycle from bid through billing, eliminating fragmented workflows & control gaps.
Built standardized business case & pricing framework, replacing ad hoc estimates with finance-supported, auditable models.
Implemented first budgeting & approval framework for acquired entities, creating a proactive expense management process. 
Standardized cross-entity reporting, eliminating duplication & reducing errors by ~50% while strengthening data governance.
Redesigned forecasting & revenue-tracking processes to improve margin, pipeline, & cash-flow visibility.
Partner with IT to translate current- & future-state processes into technical specifications, enabling ERP automation across pricing, forecasting, & billing.
Serve as primary liaison between operations & leadership, driving change management & cross-functional alignment.
Developing a scalable post-merger integration playbook to support future acquisitions & system rollouts.
Ernst & Young											                        Munich, Germany
US Real Estate Tax Compliance Analyst at EY-Steuerberatungsgesellschaft			                                    Jan 2024– Aug 2024
Supported U.S. - German tax compliance for corporate & partnership clients with > €200M in assets, ensuring accuracy across cross-border reporting requirements.
Redesigned onboarding materials & peer training workflows, accelerating new-hire ramp-up & improving team productivity.
Coordinated across international teams to resolve complex cross-border compliance tax issues, improving turnaround time by 30% & improving team productivity.
Tip-Top Industrial Solutions 								  	                           Berlin, Germany
Merger Organization & Process Specialist						                                                May 2016 – Aug 2023
Supported post-merger organizational & proves integration for LAWA/WKH, aligning budgeting & reporting structures with parent-company standards. 
Documented & standardized ERP workflows to support system transition & staff onboarding.
Performed budget variance analysis 24M+ RUB infrastructure projects, partnering with senior leadership in decision making.
Spearheaded ERP (1C) implementation, helping automate workflows, develop training materials & organizing system documentation. 
EDUCATION

Northeastern University | D’Amore-McKim School of Business – Boston, MA	                                                                      Dec 2024
Bachelor of Science in International Business: Finance | Minor in History   					                       cum laude 
Honors: Dean’s List (Fall ’24, Spring ‘23, Fall ‘22, Spring ’21)
SKILLS & INTERESTS

Tools: Excel (X/V Lookup, Index Match, Pivot Tables & Charts, Sum/Count/Average Ifs, Power Query, Data Validation, Scenario Tables & Forecast Models). Financial & Process Systems (ERP (1C), GoSystems, CSV/API/ASCII Integrations)
Languages: English (Native), Russian (Fluent), German (Intermediate), Spanish (Beginner) 


13. Anything else we should know about your situation?	I am looking to move into a more clearly defined/specialized role where I can build depth in a specific skill set. Righ now, I do a little bit of everything and it is way too crazy to feel like I am really getting a skill set. I learn best in environments with strong peer groups, mentorship, and opportunities for collaboration. I want to work on teams with a younger, growth-oriented culture where learning, feedback, and professional development are part of the day-to-day, and there is a clear path for progression.
Terms And Conditions	I understand that WRNSignal is designed to guide decision-making, prioritization, and strategy. It does not auto-apply to jobs and may recommend not applying to certain roles.

jobText:
Job description
Position: Senior Analyst, Finance Transformation - Process - Central
##
• * Current Employees:
• * If you are a current Staff, Faculty or Temporary employee at the University of Miami, please click to log in to Workday to use the internal application process. To learn how to apply for a faculty or staff position using the Career worklet, please review this .The UHealth system at the University of Miami has an exciting opportunity for a full time Senior Analyst, Finance Transformation - Process in the UHealth Finance Department.
• * SUMMARY
• * The Senior Analyst, Finance Transformation - Process - Central (H) supports the execution of the overall finance process transformation for UHealth/MSOM through project management, performance improvement initiatives, and strategic communication. The Senior Analyst, Finance Transformation – Process – Central (H) enables efficient and effective finance operations by planning, implementing, and tracking a variety of projects and initiatives to ensure their successful development, completion, and sustainability.

Moreover, the incumbent holds responsibility for evaluating current processes and making recommendations for improvement, while ensuring a solid communication strategy to keep all stakeholders involved and informed.
• * CORE JOB FUNCTIONS
• ** Works with various Finance teams to identify areas of opportunity and define the scope of projects/initiatives designed to improve finance effectiveness and efficiency at UHealth/MSOM.
• Discusses the objectives and measures upon which projects/initiatives will be evaluated at completion and determines the resources required for execution.
• Establishes and documents comprehensive plans and timelines which identify and sequence the activities needed to successfully complete projects/initiatives.
• Monitors the progress of projects/initiatives and adjusts scope/strategy/timelines as needed to adapt to changes to ensure successful completion.
• Consults with the appropriate stakeholders on the selection of employees to assist with projects/initiatives, provides training and orientation, and assigns tasks as appropriate.
• Reviews the quality of work completed with the project team on a regular basis to ensure that work produced meets project standards.
• Prepares presentations on completed projects/initiatives and presents status reports to stakeholders.
• Adheres to University and unit-level policies and procedures and safeguards University assets.
• ** This list of duties and responsibilities is not intended to be all-inclusive and may be expanded to include other duties or responsibilities, as necessary.
• **** CORE QUALIFICATIONS
• **
• Education:
• * Bachelor’s degree in finance or relevant field
• * Experience:
• * Minimum 5 years of relevant experience
• * Knowledge, Skills, and Attitudes:
• ** Skill in collecting, organizing, and analyzing data.
• Ability to recognize, analyze, and solve a variety of problems.
• Ability to exercise sound judgment in making critical decisions.
• ** Any appropriate combination of relevant education, experience and/or certifications may be considered.
• ** The University of Miami offers competitive salaries and a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, tuition remission and more.
